summary:
Sternal fractures in growing children are rare due to the immature, less ossified skeletal structure that offers inherent resistance to blunt trauma. Although uncommon, these injuries warrant high suspicion for abusive causes and may be overlooked despite prompting thorough investigation in clinical practice.  

\boxed{A}
